content: Alpine.js x-for must be on a <template> with a single root element, bound with a stable :key (not the array index) so DOM nodes track correctly across add/remove/reorder. Standard dynamic-rows pattern: x-data holds an array of option objects (e.g. {id, body, is_correct}), a addOption()/removeOption(index) method push/splice the array, and each rendered input's name attribute is built with x-bind:name="'options['+index+'][body]'" (Alpine template interpolation) to produce Laravel-parseable options[0][body]/options[0][is_correct] POST field names. The single-correct radio across dynamically numbered rows is bound via x-model on a shared scalar (e.g. correctIndex) rather than per-row booleans, then serialized into options[i][is_correct] on submit — or simpler: a radio group named 'correct_index' submitted alongside the options array, letting the Form Request/controller translate a single selected index into is_correct flags for the target Option models. Sources: Alpine.js official docs (x-for), community Alpine dynamic-formset writeups (Medium/CodePen), cross-checked as consistent.
